**Content Standards**

Minor/advisory: several leaf descriptions still duplicate DAG information in prose, e.g. `8b15b679` starts with `**Prerequisite:** the template/node types and .jit/templates.toml loader (the prior task in this story) exist.` The content standard says the DAG is canonical and forbids dependency duplication/cross-references between siblings. This is not the main blocker because the issue bodies are otherwise understandable and contain verifiable `## Success Criteria`.

No blocking content-standard failure found in the current implementation leaves. Titles are clean; story labels are kebab slugs; task/story criteria are marked with `[hard]` IDs.

**Coverage Vs Design**

| Design item | Issue | Matches? |
|---|---:|---|
| W1 — template types, `.jit/templates.toml` loader, repo `plan` template, registry accessors | `04559558`, `45e7f203`, `8b15b679` | Yes |
| W2 — apply engine, interpolation, validate-before-mutate, edge wiring, `move-upstream-to-role` | `643874f2`, `14137e1a`, `73e5e853` | Yes |
| W3 — atomic scaffold switch: add `jit apply`, breakdown consumes pre-created B, remove old commands together | `3143ad87`, `9cccfe0c`, `e75805f6` | Yes |
| W4 — migrate remaining `PlanningConfig` consumers, remove flat config and examples | `96fc8cd8`, `c703a4b3`, `3a7e13cf` | Yes |
| W5 — migrate skills, memory, docs, bracket id convention | `adc640e4`, `6e38a577`, `c8aa199e` | Yes |
| W6 — comprehensive tests, property tests, dogfood | `99a0c161`, `840e155c`, `fc414353` | Mostly |
| REQ-09 — manual reseed existing empty planning descriptions, not engine deliverable | `57f52018`; aggregate `99a0c161` | Yes |

The prior W3 atomicity defect appears addressed: `9cccfe0c` is now additive only, with `SCFA-02` saying no default scaffold path changes, and `e75805f6` performs the public switch, breakdown refactor, and old-command removal together.

**Dependency DAG Correctness**

Blocking failure: W6 automated tests are still over-constrained behind W5. The approved plan says W6 has `deps: W1–W4; W5 for skill-level dogfood`. Current graph has `840e155c` depending on `adc640e4`:

```text
840e155c -> adc640e4 -> 96fc8cd8 -> ...
```

Issue `840e155c` is `Unit, integration, and property tests`; its criterion is `TSTA-01: unit, temp-repo integration, and property tests pass and never touch the production store.` These engine/CLI/config tests need W1-W4, not skills, memory, and user docs. This falsely serializes automated test work behind `adc640e4`.

Concrete fix:

```bash
jit dep rm 840e155c adc640e4
jit dep add 840e155c 3a7e13cf
```

Blocking failure: the manual reseed task is also over-constrained behind automated tests and W5. Current graph has:

```text
57f52018 -> 840e155c -> adc640e4 -> ...
```

Issue `57f52018` says it is a “one-time manual data repair, not an engine feature.” The plan’s “Backfill” decision says manual reseed is “not engine,” and W6 only requires W5 for skill-level dogfood. Reseeding `1eb0bdfd`/`2937919f` should not wait for property tests or docs/skills migration.

Concrete fix:

```bash
jit dep rm 57f52018 840e155c
jit dep add 57f52018 3a7e13cf
```

Blank-workspace reachability: the graph-template implementation root is `45e7f203`, which depends on the breakdown node and can begin on a fresh workspace after this breakdown gate. No false ready root found.

**Structural Integrity**

The story layer is present and W1-W5 decomposition is operationally coherent. W3’s atomic split is now structurally acceptable: additive command plumbing first, then one atomic switch leaf.

Blocking structural issue remains in W6: the structure routes all tests and the manual reseed through the skills/docs story, even though the approved plan only requires W5 for skill-level dogfood. This makes the W6 story less parallel than the design allows and keeps prior dependency feedback unresolved.

Concrete fix: apply the two DAG changes above. If skill-level dogfood is desired, add a separate child under `99a0c161`:

```bash
jit issue create --type task --title "Dogfood apply plan through migrated skills" --label epic:graph-templates --label story:template-testing-dogfood --label milestone:v1.0 --label satisfies:REQ-08
jit dep add <new-skill-dogfood-task> adc640e4
jit dep add <new-skill-dogfood-task> 840e155c
```

**Prior Review Feedback**

Most recent run `6dac5a9a` had two blocking findings. The W3 atomicity finding was addressed by rewriting `9cccfe0c` as additive and `e75805f6` as the atomic switch. The W6 over-constraint remains: `840e155c` still depends on `adc640e4`. A related over-constraint is visible now in `57f52018 -> 840e155c -> adc640e4`.

VERDICT: FAIL
